content.full_text:
MEMBERS
RUBEN S. AYALA
VICE CHAIR
CHARLES M. CALDERON
WILLIAM A. CRAVEN
CECIL GREEN
FRANK HILL
QUENTIN KOSK
ROBERT PRESLEY
NEWTON R. RUSSELL

CONSULTANTS
PETER M. DETWILER
LESLIE A. MCFADDEN
COMMITTEE SECRETARY
KAYE PACKARD
ROOM 2085
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 94248
SACRAMENTO 94248-0001
(916) 445-9748

California Legislature
Senate Committee on Local Government
MARIAN BERGESON
CHAIRMAN

January 15, 1991

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing to request your formal advice on my own behalf. After discussing two questions with Jeanne Pritchard and Carla Wardlow, I am seeking your written advice.

Question 1: I have been invited to serve as one of five executive editors of a proposed publication to be called The California Land Use Quarterly. A Journal of Land Use Law and Social Policy. The California Continuing Education of the Bar, operated by the Regents of the University of California through UC Extension, will publish the Land Use Quarterly. The proposed Quarterly will cover housing, land use planning, environmental quality, and open space issues, all topics which fall within the Senate Local Government Committee's jurisdiction. I understand that the editorial board will meet quarterly to set editorial policy, guide the publication's professional staff, and to review materials. As a member of the editorial board, I would not receive any compensation for my service except for reimbursement of my actual travel expenses. I intend to take time off from my position as the Committee's consultant to attend the editorial board meetings. What requirements of the ethics laws should I keep in mind?

Question 2: I have been invited to moderate a panel at a national conference in Washington D.C. sponsored by the American Farmland Trust. The American Farmland Trust, a private nonprofit organization founded in 1980, is nationally known for its efforts to conserve agricultural land. My panel, "State Tax Incentives for Farmland Protection," involves issues which fall within the Senate Local Government Commit-

tee's jurisdiction. I will help AFT identify the other panelists, I will coordinate their presentations, and I will comment on their remarks. AFT has offered to pay up to $300 towards my travel and lodging expenses. I intend to ask the Senate Rules Committee to pay for the balance. I will participate as a Senate staff member, not on leave time. What requirements of the ethics law should I keep in mind?

I would appreciate having your prompt response to these questions so I can arrange my participation. If you or your staff need additional information about these activities, please call me directly. Thank you in advance for your careful consideration of my requests.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Peter M. Detwiler

cc: Jeanne Pritchard
Ann Bailey
